title: No cloning with unitary scaling
abstract: It is known that the classical information like strings of bits can be copied. In 1982, Wootters and Zurek proposed the quantum no-cloning principle [1]. No-cloning principle says that it is impossible to make an identical copy of an arbitrary unknown quantum pure state by using unitary evolution. In this paper, we propose a general no-cloning principle. We call U|psi>, where U is any unitary operator, a U-copy of the state |psi>. The general no-cloning principle states that it is impossible to make a U-copy of an arbitrary unknown quantum pure state by using unitary evolution.
summary: This paper proposes a generalization of the fundamental quantum no-cloning principle. Instead of looking at identical copies, it examines the possibility of creating copies that have been transformed by a unitary operator U. The author proves that such 'U-copying' is impossible for arbitrary unknown states, effectively showing that the constraints of the original no-cloning theorem extend to any unitary scaling of the state.
main_problem: The paper investigates whether it is possible to create a 'U-copy' of an unknown quantum pure state, where the copy is the original state transformed by an arbitrary unitary operator U, using unitary evolution.
main_result: The author proves a general no-cloning principle stating that it is impossible to create a U-copy of an arbitrary unknown pure state via unitary evolution, establishing that U-copying is only possible for identical or orthogonal states.
method: The proof utilizes the inner product method to compare the transformation of states under unitary evolution and employs linearity tests and counter-examples using CNOT gates.
model_or_system: The model involves an unknown quantum pure state |psi> and a target system initially in a ready state |0>, subjected to a unitary transformation T designed to produce the state |psi>U|psi>.
important_parameters: The identity of the unitary operator U and the inner product between states |psi> and |phi>.
main_assumptions: The proof assumes that the copying process is governed by unitary evolution and focuses specifically on unknown pure states.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the concept of U-copying, provides an analytical proof using inner products, demonstrates failure via a CNOT gate counter-example, performs a linearity test, and concludes with an equivalence theorem to standard cloning.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
